terms.def: Malignancy of the prostate gland; the most common non-skin cancer in men.
Prostate cancer confined to the prostate, with no detectable spread.
Rising PSA after definitive therapy without imaging-detectable disease.
Metastatic Hormone-Sensitive Prostate Cancer — disease that still responds to androgen deprivation.
Metastatic Castration-Resistant Prostate Cancer — progresses despite androgen deprivation therapy.
Tumor / Node / Metastasis — standard cancer staging system describing primary tumor, lymph node involvement, and distant metastases.
Limited number of metastases (typically ≤3–5), often considered for targeted local therapy.
Prostate-Specific Antigen — blood biomarker used to screen, stage, and monitor prostate cancer activity.
Prostate-Specific Membrane Antigen — protein highly expressed on prostate cancer cells and the molecular target for PSMA imaging and theranostics.
Androgen Receptor and its splice variant AR-V7; AR-V7 is associated with resistance to androgen receptor-targeted therapies.
Positron Emission Tomography — functional/molecular imaging that detects gamma rays from positron-emitting radiotracers.
Computed Tomography — anatomical X-ray-based cross-sectional imaging.
Magnetic Resonance Imaging — soft tissue imaging using magnetic fields and radio waves.
Single Photon Emission Computed Tomography — nuclear imaging using gamma-emitting tracers.
Hybrid scanner combining functional PET with anatomical CT — the standard modality for PSMA imaging.
Hybrid scanner combining PET with MRI — used in select centers for higher soft-tissue contrast.
Whole-Body PET — imaging from skull base to mid-thigh (or eyes-to-thighs) for systemic disease assessment.
Standardized Uptake Value — normalized measure of radiotracer uptake corrected for injected dose and patient weight.
Maximum SUV in a lesion (single hottest voxel) — most commonly reported quantitative metric.
Mean SUV across a defined region of interest.
Mean SUV in a small fixed-size sphere (typically 1 cm³) around the hottest voxel — less noise-sensitive than SUVmax.
Metabolic Tumor Volume — total volume of metabolically active tumor.
Total Lesion Glycolysis (FDG) or Total Lesion Uptake (PSMA analog) — MTV multiplied by SUVmean.
Aggregate volumetric or uptake measure summed across all detected lesions.
Gallium-68 labeled PSMA tracer for PET imaging. Brand names include Locametz (Novartis) and Illuccix (Telix).
Pylarify (Lantheus) — FDA-approved fluorine-18 PSMA tracer with longer half-life than Ga-68 variants.
Fluorine-18 PSMA tracer with delayed renal clearance, useful for pelvic imaging.
Posluma (Blue Earth Diagnostics) — FDA-approved fluorine-18 PSMA tracer.
Fluorodeoxyglucose — general oncology PET tracer measuring glucose metabolism (not PSMA-specific).
Therapy + diagnostics paired pathway: an imaging tracer identifies a molecular target, and a matched therapeutic delivers radiation to that same target.
Radioligand Therapy — systemic targeted radiation delivered via a ligand that binds to tumor antigens.
Radiopharmaceutical Therapy — broader umbrella term often used interchangeably with RLT.
Pluvicto (Novartis) — FDA-approved Lutetium-177 PSMA radioligand therapy for mCRPC.
Actinium-225 PSMA — alpha-emitting radioligand therapy, currently investigational.
Radionuclide emitting beta particles (e.g., Lu-177) — longer tissue range, lower linear energy transfer (LET).
Radionuclide emitting alpha particles (e.g., Ac-225) — short range, very high LET, more cytotoxic per decay.
Response Evaluation Criteria In Solid Tumors — anatomical (CT/MRI) response framework based on lesion size changes.
PET Response Criteria In Solid Tumors — quantitative PET-based response framework using SUL changes.
Prostate Cancer Working Group response criteria; PCWG4 is the latest update incorporating PSMA imaging.
Response Evaluation Criteria In PSMA-PET/CT — emerging PSMA-specific response framework.
Following individual lesions across imaging timepoints to characterize response heterogeneity (TrackPSMA's approach).
Differences in interpretation between different readers (Nucs AI's preferred term over 'subjectivity').
The same reader's interpretation differing across separate reads of the same case.
Preferred terms for non-tumor uptake on PSMA imaging (avoid 'benign uptake').
Delineating tumor or organ boundaries on imaging — manual or automated.
AI-automated segmentation of tumors or organs.
Time required for a clinician to complete an imaging report — a key efficiency metric.
Quantitative tumor burden measurement summed across the entire scan.

Nucs AI's tumor detection, TNM staging, and whole-body tumor burden quantification on PSMA PET/CT. Reported metrics: 95% tumor detection, 87% TNM staging, 99% whole-body quantification, 70% reduction in reporting time.
Nucs AI's pre-therapy responder/non-responder identification for PSMA RLT. 94% NPV; multi-center validated in US/EU.
Nucs AI's standardized response assessment with lesion-by-lesion tracking across imaging timepoints.
Phase III trial that established efficacy of Lu-177-PSMA-617 in mCRPC and led to Pluvicto's FDA approval.
Australian randomized trial comparing Lu-177-PSMA-617 vs cabazitaxel in mCRPC.
Trial of Lu-177-PSMA-617 in pre-chemotherapy mCRPC.
Australian trial that established PSMA PET/CT superiority over conventional imaging for primary staging.
Trials evaluating PSMA PET/CT in primary diagnosis and risk stratification of prostate cancer.
Study referenced internally regarding PSMA PET use around biopsy decisions.
Software as a Medical Device — software that performs medical functions without being part of a hardware device.
FDA premarket notification pathway demonstrating substantial equivalence to a legally marketed predicate device.
FDA pathway for novel low-to-moderate-risk devices that lack a predicate.
Premarket Approval — most rigorous FDA pathway, required for Class III devices.
Investigational Device Exemption — FDA mechanism allowing clinical study of an unapproved device.
Humanitarian Device Exemption — FDA pathway for devices treating rare conditions.
FDA Quality System Regulation (QSR) governing medical device manufacturers.
Quality Management System Regulation — FDA's update aligning 21 CFR 820 with ISO 13485 (effective 2026).
International quality management system standard for medical device organizations.
European Medical Device Regulation governing medical devices placed on the EU market.
EU In Vitro Diagnostic Regulation (2017/746) — counterpart to MDR for IVDs.
Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act — US patient health data privacy law.
General Data Protection Regulation — EU data protection and privacy law.
Institutional Review Board — ethics committee overseeing human subjects research.
Electronic Quality Management System (Nucs AI uses Greenlight Guru).
Design History File — compilation of records describing the design history of a finished device.
Device Master Record — documentation defining the procedures and specifications for a finished device.
Device History Record — record of production history for each unit of a finished device.
Standard Operating Procedure — documented process governing a recurring activity.
Form — controlled work form (e.g., FRM-07 supplier evaluation, FRM-08 job description).
Job Description — QMS-controlled document defining role responsibilities and qualifications.
Supplier Evaluation — QMS-controlled assessment of a supplier's capability and risk tier.
Non-Disclosure Agreement.
Data Processing Agreement — required under GDPR when sharing personal data with processors.
Business Associate Agreement — required under HIPAA when sharing PHI with vendors.
Service Organization Control 2 — security, availability, and confidentiality audit framework.
International information security management system standard.
Key Opinion Leader — influential physician or researcher whose endorsement carries clinical weight.
Medical Advisory Board — Nucs AI's MAB includes Drs. Calais (UCLA), Ceci (IEO Milan), Becker (Rutgers), and Nawaz (AstraZeneca).
An agreement restricting press coverage until a specified release time.
Tying brand commentary or content to breaking industry news to ride existing media attention.
Opinion editorial published under a named author, typically in a trade or general publication.
Hard news suitable for a press release. Nucs AI categories: Product, Partners, Data, Growth, Talent.
Marketing/PR activity timed to a conference's hype cycle without physically attending.
Society of Nuclear Medicine and Molecular Imaging — primary US nuclear medicine professional society.
European Association of Nuclear Medicine — primary European nuclear medicine society.
Radiological Society of North America — largest annual radiology conference.
American Society of Clinical Oncology — major oncology conference.
European Society for Medical Oncology — major European oncology conference.
European Association of Urology.
American Urological Association.
Journal of Nuclear Medicine — flagship journal of SNMMI.
European Journal of Nuclear Medicine and Molecular Imaging — flagship EANM journal.
Clinical Nuclear Medicine.
Journal of Clinical Oncology — flagship ASCO journal.
European Urology — top-tier urology journal, EAU's flagship.
Lantheus subsidiary providing aPROMISE — auto-segmentation and PSMA reporting software.
Imaging AI portfolio that includes PSMA workflow tools.
Manufacturer of Pluvicto (Lu-177-PSMA-617) and Locametz — primary RLT pharma player in PSMA.
Manufacturer of Pylarify (18F-DCFPyL) and parent of Exini.
Manufacturer of Posluma (18F-rhPSMA-7.3).
Strategic partner in oncology pipeline development.
